news.title: Lattice Semiconductor Announces Nexus 2 Platform with Advanced AI Capabilities
news.summary: Next-generation FPGA platform delivers 3x performance improvement and enhanced AI/ML acceleration for edge computing applications.
news.content:
Lattice Semiconductor Corporation today announced the Lattice Nexus 2 platform, the next generation of its industry-leading low-power FPGA architecture. Built on an advanced process node, Nexus 2 delivers up to 3x the performance of the previous generation while maintaining the ultra-low power consumption that Lattice is known for.

Key features of the Nexus 2 platform include:
- Enhanced AI/ML acceleration engines supporting up to 50 TOPS/W
- Improved security features including post-quantum cryptography support
- Expanded connectivity options with PCIe Gen4 and 25G Ethernet
- Backward compatibility with existing Nexus designs

The first devices in the Nexus 2 family are expected to sample in Q4 2024, with production availability in early 2025.
news.author: Lattice Newsroom

news.title: Lattice Releases sensAI 4.0 with Advanced Model Optimization
news.summary: Latest version of sensAI solution stack brings 40% improvement in inference performance and support for transformer-based models.
news.content:
Lattice Semiconductor has released version 4.0 of its sensAI solution stack for edge AI applications. This major update introduces significant improvements in model optimization and expands support for modern neural network architectures.

New features in sensAI 4.0:
- Support for transformer-based models including Vision Transformers
- Advanced quantization algorithms achieving 40% better inference performance
- New model zoo with pre-optimized reference designs
- Enhanced integration with popular ML frameworks

The update is available now as a free download for registered Lattice developers.
news.author: Lattice AI Team

news.title: Major Automotive OEM Selects Lattice FPGAs for ADAS Platform
news.summary: Leading European automaker chooses Lattice CertusPro-NX FPGAs for next-generation advanced driver assistance systems.
news.content:
Lattice Semiconductor today announced that a major European automotive OEM has selected Lattice CertusPro-NX FPGAs for its next-generation ADAS platform. The design win represents a significant milestone for Lattice in the automotive market.

The Lattice FPGAs will be used for:
- Camera interface aggregation and processing
- Sensor fusion from multiple radar and lidar units
- Real-time AI inference for object detection
- Functional safety monitoring

This design win is expected to generate significant revenue starting in 2025 as the vehicle platform enters production.
news.author: Lattice Automotive Team

news.title: Lattice Security Solutions Achieve Common Criteria Certification
news.summary: Certus-NX and MachXO5-NX FPGAs with Sentry stack receive EAL4+ certification for security-critical applications.
news.content:
Lattice Semiconductor announced today that its security solutions based on Certus-NX and MachXO5-NX FPGAs have achieved Common Criteria Evaluation Assurance Level 4+ (EAL4+) certification. This certification validates the robustness of Lattice's hardware security features for use in government and critical infrastructure applications.

The certification covers:
- Hardware Root of Trust implementation
- Secure boot and firmware protection
- Cryptographic module validation
- Tamper detection and response

This achievement positions Lattice as a leading provider of secure FPGA solutions for applications requiring the highest levels of security assurance.
news.author: Lattice Security Team
